content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 13,
1994
Robert 0.
Cook
South Pasadena Taxpayers Assn.
324 Hawthorne Street
South Pasadena,
CA
91030
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1-94-130
Dear Mr.
Cook:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding the responsibilities of members of the South Pasadena
City Council pursuant to the conflict—of-interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
You have asked whether the
members of the South Pasadena City Council may decide whether to
have the city pay punitive damages awarded to Lee Prentiss, if the
court should award punitive damages to Mr. Prentiss against the
councilmembers as individuals.
The Act requires the Commission to provide formal written
advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or
by that person’s authorized representative.
(Section 83114(b).)
The Commission does not provide advice to third parties concerning
another person’s duties under the Act.
- (Regulation 18329(b) (8),
copy enclosed.)
It does not appear from your letter that you are acting bn
behalf of the South Pasadena City Council.
We do not give third
party advice.
(Regulation 18329(b) (8).)
Consequently,
we are
unable to provide advice on the issue you raised in your letter.
However,
we have enclosed some prior advice letters for your
information.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000-18954.
All references to Regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
1-94-130
Page
2
Please note, however, that while these advice letters are
useful for general guidance,
Commission written advice is the
application of the law to a particular set of facts provided by
the requestor.
Thus, the conclusions in the letters are limited
to the specific facts contained therein.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)322—5901.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
1 Counsel
John W. Wallace
Counsel,
Legal Division
ENCLOSURES

sections.question: Whether the members of the South Pasadena City Council may decide whether to have the city pay punitive damages awarded to Lee Prentiss, if the court should award punitive damages to Mr. Prentiss against the councilmembers as individuals.
sections.conclusion: The Commission does not provide advice to third parties concerning another person's duties under the Act. It does not appear from your letter that you are acting on behalf of the South Pasadena City Council. Consequently, we are unable to provide advice on the issue you raised in your letter.
sections.question_synthetic: Can South Pasadena City Council members decide whether the city should pay punitive damages awarded against them individually in a lawsuit?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requester is a third party (South Pasadena Taxpayers Association) rather than the City Council itself, and the Commission does not provide third-party advice under Regulation 18329(b)(8).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter. The FPPC explicitly states it cannot provide advice to third parties and cites specific regulatory authority. No formal conclusion on the merits was provided since the request was deemed outside the Commission's scope.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the members of the South Pasadena City Council may decide whether to have the city pay punitive damages awarded to Lee Prentiss, if the court should award punitive damages to Mr. Prentiss against the councilmembers as individuals.

CONCLUSION: The Commission does not provide advice to third parties concerning another person's duties under the Act. It does not appear from your letter that you are acting on behalf of the South Pasadena City Council. Consequently, we are unable to provide advice on the issue you raised in your letter.

The Act requires the Commission to provide formal written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or by that person’s authorized representative. (Section 83114(b).) The Commission does not provide advice to third parties concerning another person’s duties under the Act. - (Regulation 18329(b) (8), copy enclosed.) It does not appear from your letter that you are acting bn behalf of the South Pasadena City Council. We do not give third party advice. (Regulation 18329(b) (8).) embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide informal advice to the South Pasadena Taxpayers Association regarding City Council members' conflict-of-interest duties, citing its policy against providing third-party advice. The Commission enclosed prior advice letters for general guidance.
